items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects and spending
items.summary: The council held a study session to examine Mountain View's planned capital projects for the next fiscal year. This is where residents can learn what infrastructure, facilities, or development projects the city is prioritizing with public funds.

items.headline: City Council to Revise Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program through a municipal code amendment. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations for current and future tenants in the city.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan under review
items.summary: The City Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which will shape future improvements and services at San José libraries. This plan could affect where libraries are located, what services they offer, and how residents experience them.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus square footage for seismic work
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street's historic designation from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for the owner to conduct seismic retrofitting and historic rehabilitation. This is a public hearing decision.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project requires City Council approval and represents a significant change to that commercial corridor.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8 with no new hearing date set. Residents interested in this development will need to wait for a new notice before the project can be reviewed.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully specified in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what construction project this involves and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project advances with consultant agreement
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the contract for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating ongoing work to improve safety and traffic flow at this major intersection. This project addresses conflicts between highway traffic and railroad crossings on Bascom Avenue.
